sourceMovie.title: The Mortal Instruments: City of Bones
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Mortal Instruments: City of Bones (2013) as a fantasy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The fantasy and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: I Still See You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Mortal Instruments: City of Bones clicked for you, I Still See You (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Mortal Instruments: City of Bones?
faq.answer: Try I Still See You, Mortal, Abigail—each shares DNA with The Mortal Instruments: City of Bones while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Mortal Instruments: City of Bones?
faq.answer: The Mortal Instruments: City of Bones (2013) is best described as Fantasy, Action, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Mortal Instruments: City of Bones worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
